Concept type: term
Name: Exhaustion Risk
Description: Risk defined in two ways: by price (when a trade becomes crowded at new highs/lows with no opposing positions left to stop out, requiring aggressive buyers/sellers to continue) and by time (when a new continuity level opens, such as a new day potentially negating the prior day's signal).

Respond with ONLY a single decimal number between 0.0 and 1.0. No explanation.

Concept type: fact
Name: exhaustion_by_time_favors_longer_signals
Description: Time exhaustion is why longer timeframe signals (weekly, monthly, quarterly, yearly) are preferred - they last all week/month/quarter/year. A daily signal only lasts one day before exhaustion risk from the next day's open.

Respond with ONLY a single decimal number between 0.0 and 1.0. No explanation.

Concept type: fact
Name: broadening_formation_restarts_inside_range
Description: When price gets stuck inside a previous range (inside bar on higher timeframe), it restarts the price discovery broadening formation sequence within that range. This internal broadening formation can be used to time the eventual breakout.

Respond with ONLY a single decimal number between 0.0 and 1.0. No explanation.

Concept type: fact
Name: ftfc_not_from_bottom_of_range_misconception
Description: It IS possible to have full timeframe continuity from the bottom or top of a range even when price was previously going in the opposite direction. This is a common misconception. The timing of decoupling (when new period opens occur) is what enables this.

Respond with ONLY a single decimal number between 0.0 and 1.0. No explanation.

Name: if_signal_works_what_does_it_do
Description: The most important question to ask out loud while trading: 'If this signal works, what will it create, negate, or do to the higher timeframe?' This chains daily→weekly→monthly→quarterly analysis.
